experiment: B19: B2 architecture + candidate-weighted flip consistency
note: B19 keeps the exact B2 architecture and adds a mild horizontal-flip consistency regularizer. The consistency term is weighted toward B2 candidate and uncertain pixels so sparse foreground is not overwhelmed by easy background.

design_references.title: Mean teachers are better role models / consistency regularization line
design_references.adopted_idea: prediction consistency under perturbations can improve generalization without changing architecture

design_references.title: When CNNs Outperform Transformers and Mambas
design_references.adopted_idea: for dental caries segmentation, lightweight CNN priors and robust training can be preferable to heavier modules

design_references.title: PRAD periapical radiograph benchmark
design_references.adopted_idea: periapical radiographs contain artifacts and resolution limits, so invariance to benign geometric perturbation is valuable

failure_reflection.b10_b16_skip_modules: skip/detail modules did not beat B2, suggesting more structural fusion is not the bottleneck
failure_reflection.b12_b15_rescue_heads: positive residual heads often traded recall/precision without improving global Dice
failure_reflection.b17_wavelet: validation improvement did not transfer to test, suggesting robustness/generalization needs attention
failure_reflection.b19_hypothesis: mild candidate-weighted flip consistency improves B2 robustness while preserving its architecture
